RÄvaá¹a, the mighty king of Lanka and a great devotee of Lord Shiva, was also a highly accomplished musician. To express his deep devotion, he decided to craft a veena (a classical string instrument) that could produce divine music worthy of Shiva.

He used one of his own heads as the resonating gourd (the base of the instrument).

His ten heads symbolized the ten strings or the ten qualities required to master music.

He used his tendons as the strings of the veena.

His arms formed the structure of the instrument.

With this self-made veena, he played mesmerizing music to glorify Rudra (Shiva) â hence the instrument came to be called âRudra Veena.â

This act represents both RÄvaá¹aâs intense bhakti (devotion) and his mastery over art and knowledge. Itâs a powerful symbolic tale showing that true devotion often involves total surrender of the self.
